text.title: EU AI Act Compliance — Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: Does the system match any Article 5 prohibited practice (social scoring, untargeted scraping, emotion recognition in workplace/education, real-time remote biometric ID in public spaces)?

branch.conclusion: Stop. Do not deploy. Article 5 has no proportionality test.

branch.question: Does the primary purpose map to an Annex III domain (biometrics, critical infra, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, justice)?

branch.conclusion: Treat as high-risk. Apply Articles 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 17, 43. Register in the EU AI database before deployment.
Treat as GPAI. Publish model card + training-data summary; >10^25 FLOPs adds red-team + incident reporting.

branch.when: annex_iii_match == false AND is_gpai == false AND interacts_with_users == true
branch.conclusion: Treat as limited-risk. Apply Article 50 transparency duties.

branch.when: annex_iii_match == false AND is_gpai == false AND interacts_with_users == false
branch.conclusion: Treat as minimal-risk. Document the classification rationale.

example.p: HR-screening tool processing CVs in EU → not Article 5 → Annex III(4) employment → high-risk → conclusion r3-annex-iii-high-risk.
